axiom: Context is signal; stale context is noise. The longer a session runs, the more likely yesterday's good context is today's bad bias.

operations.trigger: Any of: (a) topic shift inside the session, (b) framework warning that context window is N% full, (c) model repeats earlier mistakes after you said 'don't', (d) model references a file/decision you already removed, (e) model contradicts an explicit instruction in the LAST message but obeys an OLDER one.
operations.action: Stop. Do not edit the prompt, do not retry, do not swap model. Name the symptom out loud (one line: 'model is still using <stale fact>'). Then choose op-002 (save+clear), op-003 (fresh thread), or op-004 (partial clear).
operations.output: Symptom named. Hygiene action chosen. The trap avoided: 'just rephrasing' a prompt against a polluted history.
operations.limitations: Detection is heuristic; you will sometimes clear when you didn't need to. Acceptable cost vs. the cost of running a long session on stale context.

operations.trigger: You want to keep working on the same topic but the chat history is polluted (failed attempts, dead ends, intermediate scratchwork).
operations.action: (1) Have the model produce a single-paragraph summary of decisions made + state of the world (files touched, tests passing, open questions). (2) Copy that summary to a scratch file or clipboard. (3) Issue the framework's clear command (Aider /clear, Claude Code /clear, ChatGPT 'New chat', CrewAI re-instantiate Crew with memory=False, LangGraph new thread_id). (4) Paste the summary back as the first message of the new session.
operations.output: Fresh history with only the distilled state. Files/working tree untouched (for code tools). Cost ~30 seconds + one LM call for the summary.
operations.limitations: The summary is lossy. If the bug you're hunting depends on a specific phrase the model said three turns ago, save the raw transcript instead.
evidence: Aider SOP §6 'Context hygiene' table line 'topic switch → /clear'

operations.trigger: Topic has fully changed (new feature, new bug, new task) AND nothing from the old session is needed.
operations.action: Start a genuinely new session. Aider: exit and re-launch (or /reset). Claude Code: /clear (or exit). ChatGPT/Gemini: New chat button. CrewAI: new Crew() instance, do NOT reuse the previous one even with memory=False (agent.tools_results may persist). LangGraph: new thread_id in the config (do not reuse the old uuid).
operations.output: Zero context bleed. Maximum signal-to-noise on the new task. Cheapest possible context window.
operations.limitations: You lose access to past decisions. If the next task is even loosely related, op-002 is usually better.
evidence: docs.crewai.com/en/concepts/memory (memory must be explicitly enabled to persist across kickoffs)

operations.trigger: History is mostly good but a specific subset is polluting (e.g., one big paste of wrong code, a 5k-token failed search result, a tool output you no longer need).
operations.action: Aider: /drop <file> to remove a file from the working set without clearing chat; /tokens to confirm reduction. Claude Code: /context to inspect, then /clear sub-sections if available (else fall back to op-002). LangGraph: graph.update_state(config, {'messages': trimmed_list}) — overwrite the messages key with a filtered slice. CrewAI: re-create the offending agent only (not the whole Crew) so its short-term memory resets.
operations.output: Bad context excised; good context preserved. Lower-cost alternative to a full clear.
operations.limitations: Only some frameworks support fine-grained edits (LangGraph is best; Aider is file-level only; ChatGPT/Gemini are all-or-nothing).

operations.trigger: Aider session: token count over 25k OR topic switch OR repeated 'SEARCH block not found' errors.
operations.action: /tokens to confirm bloat. /drop <files> to shed unused files. /clear to wipe chat history (keeps /add'd files). /reset to wipe both files and history. /map to verify repo-map size.
operations.output: Token budget back under 25k; model edit-accuracy restored.
operations.limitations: /clear keeps the file list — if files themselves are the problem, use /reset.
evidence: aider.chat/docs/troubleshooting/edit-errors.html ('Above about 25k tokens, most models become distracted')

operations.trigger: Claude Code session: topic shift, context-window warning, or starting an unrelated task in the same terminal.
operations.action: /clear to reset the conversation history (preserves working directory and project files). /context to inspect what's currently loaded. Exit the CLI entirely for a hard reset (loses anything not committed to disk).
operations.output: Fresh conversation, same project state on disk.
operations.limitations: /clear is irreversible — once cleared, the model cannot recall earlier turns. Save anything important first (op-002).

operations.trigger: Debugging a CrewAI crew where outputs look biased by a previous kickoff, OR you're not sure if memory is on, OR traces are unreadable.
operations.action: Set memory=False on the Crew during debug. Re-instantiate the Crew object between runs (do not reuse). For per-agent state, instantiate a fresh Agent. If memory=True is required in prod, clear the memory store: rm -rf <storage_dir> (default LanceDB lives under ~/.crewai/) before re-running.
operations.output: Deterministic, low-noise traces. Easier to attribute bad outputs to prompt vs. memory recall.
operations.limitations: Disabling memory loses cross-kickoff context — only do this in debug, not prod (if prod actually needs it).
evidence: docs.crewai.com/en/concepts/memory (memory is opt-in; short_term + entity run extra LLM calls when enabled)
crewai SOP DC-4 ('调试期一律 memory=False')

operations.trigger: LangGraph thread is producing wrong outputs and the message history is suspect, OR you want to fork a clean run.
operations.action: Pick one: (a) New thread_id — config={'configurable': {'thread_id': str(uuid4())}} — and re-invoke from scratch. (b) graph.update_state(config, {'messages': [...]}) to surgically rewrite the message list. (c) For subgraphs that should not share state: define a separate state schema and translate at the boundary.
operations.output: Either a clean thread (a), surgically cleaned state (b), or isolated subgraph state (c).
operations.limitations: (a) is the safest but loses checkpoint history of that thread. (b) requires you to know exactly which keys to trim.

operations.trigger: Web UI chat: long thread, model is mixing topics, projects/custom-instructions are interfering.
operations.action: Click 'New chat' for a hard reset. To preserve carry-over: ask the model to 'summarize what we decided and the final state' first, copy that paragraph, paste into the new chat as your opening message.
operations.output: Fresh window, distilled carry-over. No project state to manage (web UIs have no files).
operations.limitations: Web UIs typically cannot do partial clears. It's all-or-nothing.

anti_patterns.name: Never-clear marathon session
anti_patterns.symptom: Hours-long session, model increasingly 'forgets' instructions or invents files that were dropped 50 turns ago.
anti_patterns.fix: On any topic shift OR every ~25k tokens, op-002 (save+clear). Treat /clear as a stop-the-line tool, not a panic button.

anti_patterns.name: Reflexive clear-every-message
anti_patterns.symptom: Clearing so often the model loses useful continuity (forgets which files are in scope, re-asks questions you just answered).
anti_patterns.fix: Only clear on explicit triggers (op-001). 'Things feel off' is not a trigger; name the symptom first.

anti_patterns.name: Dumping the whole old transcript into the fresh session
anti_patterns.symptom: You /clear, then paste a 50-message log back in. You've recreated the polluted context with extra steps.
anti_patterns.fix: Paste a *summary* (op-002), not raw history. If you genuinely need raw history, you didn't need to clear.

anti_patterns.name: Clearing instead of fixing the underlying issue
anti_patterns.symptom: You /clear after every failed edit. The bug is in the prompt or the model, not the history.
anti_patterns.fix: Before clearing, run prompt-history-inspect skill. If the bug repeats after a clear, /clear is not the fix.

anti_patterns.name: Forgetting memory is on (CrewAI / persistent backends)
anti_patterns.symptom: You 'cleared' the session but the agent still recalls a fact. Memory subsystem still holds it.
anti_patterns.fix: Disable memory in debug (op-007). Or wipe the memory store explicitly.
